audit_items.proof_trace: Every free site in zmalloc.c reads all required metadata (size, oldsize) before the free call, and no freed pointer is accessed after its free/realloc call — zfree pre-reads zmalloc_size(ptr) at L198 before free(ptr) at L199; zrealloc pre-reads zmalloc_size(ptr) at L153 then uses only newptr after realloc at L154; zlibc_free and zfree_no_tcache follow the same pattern. POSIX guarantees realloc does not free the original pointer on failure, so no UAF exists even in the OOM-handler-returns path (which would be a null-ptr-deref, not UAF). No double-free pattern is present: each function path reaches exactly one free/dallocx/realloc call.
